summary:
Sotrovimab is effective for adults and children with mild to moderate COVID-19 at high risk of worsening, especially before significant viral mutations reduced other treatments' viability; however, this effect is confined to non-hospitalized, low-oxygen-requiring cases per current guidelines. It maintains a distinct role via FDA Emergency Use Authorization amid broader loss of monobodies’ activity, indicating validated benefits in target populations even if not universally applicable.  

Based solely on these two sentences:

The agent concludes that sotrovimab is effective for outpatient treatment of mild to moderate COVID-19 in high-risk individuals without oxygen needs, based on persistent FDA emergency use authorization and documented ability to prevent progression to severe disease in such cohorts.  
Despite waning efficacy over time versus newer variants, sotrovimab retains value as an approved option for select patients because it was preserved amidst widespread losses of monoclonal antibody therapies—an indicator of confirmed clinical usefulness in well-defined circumstances.
